channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: OpenClaw, the Fastest-Adopted Software Ever, Is Also a Security Blind Spot
item.description: <p>OpenClaw is already running inside enterprises, often unnoticed. Learn why banning it fails and how CISOs must shift to data-centric AI governance.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openclaw-shadow-ai-agents-enterprise-security-risks/">OpenClaw, the Fastest-Adopted Software Ever, Is Also a Security Blind Spot</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration and RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new method for exfiltrating sensitive data from artificial intelligence (AI) code execution environments using domain name system (DNS) queries. In a report published Monday, BeyondTrust revealed that Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Code Interpreter's sandbox mode permits outbound DNS queries that an attacker can exploit to enable interactive shells
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Android OS-Level Attack Bypasses Mobile Payment Security
item.description: Android’s LSPosed-based attack hijacks payment apps via runtime manipulation and SIM-binding bypass

item.title: China Probe: How a Fake Fitness Tracker Became an AI ‘Top Pick’
item.description: <p>A fake fitness tracker fooled AI chatbots in China, exposing risks of AI poisoning and prompting calls for regulation. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-tracker-tricks-chatbots-china/">China Probe: How a Fake Fitness Tracker Became an AI ‘Top Pick’</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: 'CursorJack’ Attack Path Exposes Code Execution Risk in AI Development Environment
item.description: CursorJack shows how malicious MCP deeplinks in Cursor IDE can trigger user-approved code execution

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Toast, Inc.
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tricked into manually running malicious commands to address non-existent errors, is a departure from relying on traditional methods for obtaining initial access, such as through stolen credentials
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Windows 11 Bug Crippling PCs, Blocking Access to Core Drive
item.description: <p>Microsoft says a Windows 11 issue tied to Samsung Galaxy Connect can block access to the C: drive and prevent key apps from opening.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-11-bug-c-drive-access-denied-samsung-fix/">Microsoft Confirms Windows 11 Bug Crippling PCs, Blocking Access to Core Drive</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Surge in Nation State Attacks on UK Firms Amid Cyber Warfare Fears
item.description: Armis reveals that “mutually assured disruption” is no longer preventing state-backed attacks

item.title: AI is Everywhere, But CISOs are Still Securing It with Yesterday's Skills and Tools, Study Finds
item.description: A majority of security leaders are struggling to defend AI systems with tools and skills that are not fit for the challenge, according to the AI and Adversarial Testing Benchmark Report 2026 from Pentera. The report, based on a survey of 300 US CISOs and senior security leaders, examines how organizations are securing AI infrastructure and highlights critical gaps tied to skills shortages and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Average Number of Daily API Attacks Up 113% Annually
item.description: Akamai says 87% of organizations suffered an API-related security incident last year

item.title: UK Cyber Monitoring Centre Sets Its Sights on US Expansion One Year After Launch
item.description: The US Cyber Monitoring Center should be operational in 2027, said the UK CMC leadership

item.title: South Korean Police Accidentally Post Cryptocurrency Wallet Password
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Konni Deploys EndRAT Through Phishing, Uses KakaoTalk to Propagate Malware
item.description: North Korean threat actors have been observed sending phishing to compromise targets and obtain access to a victim's KakaoTalk desktop application to distribute malicious payloads to certain contacts. The activity has been attributed by South Korean threat intelligence firm Genians to a hacking group referred to as Konni. "Initial access was achieved through a spear-phishing email disguised as a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Wing FTP Vulnerability Leaking Server Paths
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Wing FTP to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, CVE-2025-47813 (CVSS score: 4.3), is an information disclosure vulnerability that leaks the installation path of the application under certain conditions
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Unveils Industry’s Newest Intent-Based AI Security Solution to Protect Enterprise AI Agents
China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
item.description: Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Instagram Users Urged to Save Encrypted DMs Before Feature Disappears
item.description: <p>Meta will soon end Instagram’s end-to-end encrypted chats, citing low adoption and directing users to export affected messages.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-meta-instagram-end-to-end-encryption-chat-shutdown-2026/">Instagram Users Urged to Save Encrypted DMs Before Feature Disappears</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Microsoft Issues Emergency Patch for Critical Windows 11 RRAS Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Microsoft releases an out-of-band hotpatch for critical Windows 11 RRAS vulnerabilities that could allow remote code execution through malicious remote servers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-11-rras-vulnerabilities-hotpatch/">Microsoft Issues Emergency Patch for Critical Windows 11 RRAS Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
item.description: Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
item.description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek

item.title: GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projects — including Django apps, ML research code, Streamlit dashboards, and PyPI packages — by appending obfuscated code to files like setup.py, main.py, and app.py," StepSecurity said. "Anyone who runs
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Outlook and 365 Hit by Widespread Outages, Users Report Login and Email Failures
item.description: <p>Microsoft is investigating issues with Outlook and Microsoft 365 after users report login failures and missing emails, alongside known bugs in classic Outlook.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-outlook-microsoft-365-outage-login-email-issues/">Microsoft Outlook and 365 Hit by Widespread Outages, Users Report Login and Email Failures</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Android 17 Leaks Reveal Major Redesign, AI Features, and Privacy Upgrades
item.description: <p>Android 17 beta is here. Here’s what is confirmed so far, what leaks suggest, and which rumored features may arrive later in 2026.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-android-17-leaks-features-beta-release-rumors/">Android 17 Leaks Reveal Major Redesign, AI Features, and Privacy Upgrades</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Industrial Systems Under Siege: 77% of OT Environments Suffer Cyber Breaches
item.description: <p>Industrial systems face rising cyber threats as OT security lags modernization. A new survey reveals widespread breaches and growing risks to critical infrastructure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-industrial-cybersecurity-ot-security-survey/">Industrial Systems Under Siege: 77% of OT Environments Suffer Cyber Breaches</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: Researchers Warn of Global Surge in Fake Shipment Tracking Scams
item.description: Some of these campaigns are linked to Darcula, a Chinese-language phishing-as-a-service platform

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, and research that stops feeling theoretical real fast. A few bits hit a little too close to real life, too. There’s a good mix here: weird abuse of trusted stuff, quiet infrastructure ugliness,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
item.description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: CrackArmor Flaws Expose Linux Systems to Privilege Escalation
item.description: CrackArmor AppArmor flaws let local Linux users gain root, break containers and enable DoS attacks

item.title: 875 Million Android Phones Face Risk Due to Hidden Chip Flaw
item.description: <p>A MediaTek chip flaw could put up to 875 million Android phones at risk, exposing how a locked device can still be vulnerable below the surface.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-android-chip-flaw-875m/">875 Million Android Phones Face Risk Due to Hidden Chip Flaw</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Security Flaw in AWS Bedrock Code Interpreter Raises Alarms
item.description: DNS-based attack in AWS Bedrock AgentCore lets AI sandboxes exfiltrate cloud data

item.title: Truecaller Gives Families a Way to Stop Scam Calls Remotely
item.description: <p>Truecaller is turning scam defense into a shared task, giving relatives tools to manage protection settings and step into suspected fraud calls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-truecaller-family-protection-phone-scams/">Truecaller Gives Families a Way to Stop Scam Calls Remotely</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Why Security Validation Is Becoming Agentic
item.description: If you run security at any reasonably complex organization, your validation stack probably looks something like this: a BAS tool in one corner. A pentest engagement, or maybe an automated pentesting product, in another. A vulnerability scanner feeding an attack surface management platform somewhere else. Each tool gives you a slice of the picture. None of them talks to each other in any
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Spread MacSync macOS Infostealer via Fake AI Tool Installers
item.description: Three different ClickFix campaigns have been found to act as a delivery vector for the deployment of a macOS information stealer called MacSync. "Unlike traditional exploit-based attacks, this method relies entirely on user interaction – usually in the form of copying and executing commands – making it particularly effective against users who may not appreciate the implications of running
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Calls for Help to Track Steam Malware Campaign
item.description: The FBI wants to hear from gamers who have downloaded Steam titles containing malware

item.title: UK: Companies House Web Glitch Exposes Corporate Details to Fraudsters
item.description: An issue with the Companies House website has put the personal and corporate information of millions at risk

item.title: Possible New Result in Quantum Factorization
item.description: <p>I&#8217;m skeptical about&#8212;and not qualified to review&#8212;this <a href="https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202510.1649">new result</a> in factorization with a quantum computer, but if it&#8217;s true it&#8217;s a <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/quantum-decryption-of-rsa-is-much-closer-than-expected/">theoretical improvement</a> in the speed of factoring large numbers with a quantum computer.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: DRILLAPP Backdoor Targets Ukraine, Abuses Microsoft Edge Debugging for Stealth Espionage
item.description: Ukrainian entities have emerged as the target of a new campaign likely orchestrated by threat actors linked to Russia, according to a report from S2 Grupo's LAB52 threat intelligence team. The campaign, observed in February 2026, has been assessed to share overlaps with a prior campaign mounted by Laundry Bear (aka UAC-0190 or Void Blizzard) aimed at Ukrainian defense forces with a malware
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Android 17 Blocks Non-Accessibility Apps from Accessibility API to Prevent Malware Abuse
item.description: Google is testing a new security feature as part of Android Advanced Protection Mode (AAPM) that prevents certain kinds of apps from using the accessibility services API. The change, incorporated in Android 17 Beta 2, was first reported by Android Authority last week. AAPM was introduced by Google in Android 16, released last year. When enabled, it causes the device to enter a heightened
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber TPRM vs Compliance TPRM: Which is right for you? | UpGuard
item.description: Cyber TPRM and compliance TPRM answer different questions and serve different buyers. Learn how to tell them apart and decide which is best for you.

item.title: The Vendor Tiering Series: Tiering that Scales | UpGuard
item.description: An effective TPRM program begins with a logical framework. Learn how to tier vendors using weighted risk scoring to maintain a defensible program.

item.title: Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Others Unite Under New Anti-Scam Pact
item.description: <p>Google, Meta, Amazon, OpenAI, and others join a new voluntary accord to share signals, tighten safeguards, and fight online scams across platforms.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-big-tech-anti-scam-accord/">Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Others Unite Under New Anti-Scam Pact</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: OpenClaw AI Agent Flaws Could Enable Prompt Injection and Data Exfiltration
item.description: China's National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team (CNCERT) has issued a warning about the security stemming from the use of OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot and Moltbot), an open-source and self-hosted autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) agent. In a post shared on WeChat, CNCERT noted that the platform's "inherently weak default security configurations," coupled with its
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: B. Schneier

item.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions to Target Developers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that they say represents a "significant escalation" in how it propagates through the Open VSX registry. "Instead of requiring every malicious listing to embed the loader directly, the threat actor is now abusing extensionPack and extensionDependencies to turn initially standalone-looking extensions into transitive
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Fake PoCs, Misunderstood Risks Cause Cisco SD-WAN Chaos
item.description: The excitement around Cisco's latest SD-WAN bugs has inspired some light fraud, misunderstandings, and overlooked potential hazards.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Critical Chrome Security Flaws Threaten Billions of Users Worldwide
item.description: <p>Google patches two actively exploited Chrome vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to crash browsers or run malicious code. Billions of users urged to update.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-zero-day-vulnerabilities-exploited-update/">Critical Chrome Security Flaws Threaten Billions of Users Worldwide</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
item.description: Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 is tracking the threat activity under the moniker CL-STA-1087, where CL refers to cluster, and STA stands for state-backed motivation. "The activity demonstrated strategic operational patience and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Authenticator Flaw on Android, iOS Could Leak Login Codes for Millions
item.description: <p>A vulnerability in Microsoft Authenticator for Android and iOS could expose login codes to malicious apps on the same device. Microsoft has released a patch.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-authenticator-vulnerability-android-ios-login-codes/">Microsoft Authenticator Flaw on Android, iOS Could Leak Login Codes for Millions</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
item.description: Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Meta to Shut Down Instagram End-to-End Encrypted Chat Support Starting May 2026
item.description: Meta has announced plans to discontinue support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for chats on Instagram after May 8, 2026. "If you have chats that are impacted by this change, you will see instructions on how you can download any media or messages you may want to keep," the social media giant said in a help document. "If you're on an older version of Instagram, you may also need to update the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Interpol's 'Operation Synergia III' Nets 94 Arrests in Major Cybercrime Sweep
item.description: A new law enforcement operation against phishing and ransomware operators led to the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses

item.title: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
item.description: Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from Omdia shows.
item.dc:creator: Hollie Hennessy, Aaron West

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts to dismantle criminal networks, disrupt emerging threats, and safeguard victims from scams. The effort is part of an international law enforcement operation that involved 72 countries and territories.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Expansions, Cyberthreats, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 9–13.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-expansions-cyberthreats-and-industry-shifts-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Expansions, Cyberthreats, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Storm-2561 Spreads Trojan VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a credential theft campaign that employs fake virtual private network (VPN) clients distributed through search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning techniques. "The campaign redirects users searching for legitimate enterprise software to malicious ZIP files on attacker-controlled websites to deploy digitally signed trojans that masquerade as trusted VPN clients
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Investigating a New Click-Fix Variant
item.description: Disclaimer: This report has been prepared by the Threat Research Center to enhance cybersecurity awareness and support the strengthening of defense capabilities. It is based on independent research and observations of the current threat landscape available at the time of publication. The content is intended for informational and preparedness purposes only. Read more blogs around threat
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
item.description: Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations. Thanks to AI, the new top cause of compromises in the cloud is vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Real-Time Banking Trojan Strikes Brazil's Pix Users
item.description: The latest banking Trojan campaign to hit Brazil combines classic malware with a real-time human operator, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Academia and the “AI Brain Drain”
item.description: <p>In 2025, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta collectively spent US$380 billion on building artificial-intelligence tools. That number is expected to surge still higher this year, to $650 billion, to fund the building of physical infrastructure, such as data centers (see <a href="https://go.nature.com/3lzf79q">go.nature.com/3lzf79q</a>). Moreover, these firms are spending lavishly on one particular segment: top technical talent.</p> <p>Meta reportedly offered a single AI researcher, who had cofounded a start-up firm focused on training AI agents to use computers, a compensation package of $250 million over four years (see ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Face value: What it takes to fool facial recognition
item.description: ESET’s Jake Moore used smart glasses, deepfakes and face swaps to ‘hack’ widely-used facial recognition systems – and he'll demo it all at RSAC 2026

item.title: Law Enforcement Dismantles SocksEscort Proxy Network in Operation Lightning
item.description: Operation Lightning sees international law enforcement partners shut down ‘SocksEscort,’ a major malicious proxy service used by cybercriminals worldwide

item.title: Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild Affecting Skia and V8
item.description:
Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address two high-severity vulnerabilities that it said have been exploited in the wild. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2026-3909 (CVSS score: 8.8) - An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Skia 2D graphics library that allows a remote attacker to perform out-of-bounds memory access via a crafted HTML
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nine CrackArmor Flaws in Linux AppArmor Enable Root Escalation, Bypass Container Isolation
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel's AppArmor module that could be exploited by unprivileged users to circumvent kernel protections, escalate to root, and undermine container isolation guarantees. The nine confused deputy vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed CrackArmor by the Qualys Threat Research Unit (TRU). The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Exploiting 369,000 IPs Across 163 Countries
item.description: A court-authorized international law enforcement operation has dismantled a criminal proxy service named SocksEscort that enslaved thousands of residential routers worldwide into a botnet for committing large-scale fraud. "SocksEscort infected home and small business internet routers with malware," the U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said. "The malware allowed SocksEscort to direct internet
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Veeam Patches 7 Critical Backup & Replication Flaws Allowing Remote Code Execution
item.description:
Veeam has released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup &amp; Replication software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The vulnerabilities are as follows -
CVE-2026-21666 (CVSS score: 9.9) - A vulnerability that allows an authenticated domain user to perform remote code execution on the Backup Server. CVE-2026-21667 (
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Can't Wait
item.description: Organizations have to prepare to ensure they have cryptography in place in the post-quantum world.
item.dc:creator: Manju Naglapur

item.title: Iran MOIS Colludes With Criminals to Boost Cyberattacks
item.description: Iranian APTs have long pretended to be cybercriminal groups. Now they're working with actual cybercriminal groups.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Commercial Spyware Opponents Fear US Policy Shifting
item.description: Rescinded sanctions and reactivated contracts have created confusion about the Trump administration's spyware policy and where it draws the line.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: iPhones and iPads Approved for NATO Classified Data
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Security Flaw in WordPress Plugin Puts 400,000 Websites at Risk
item.description: <p>A security flaw in the Ally WordPress plugin used on more than 400,000 sites could allow attackers to extract sensitive data without logging in.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-wordpress-ally-plugin-vulnerability-400k-sites/">Security Flaw in WordPress Plugin Puts 400,000 Websites at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware families associated with the Latin American cybercrime ecosystem. The malware, which is designed to infect Windows systems and was first discovered last month, has been codenamed VENON by Brazilian
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Delinea's StrongDM Acquisition Highlights the Changing Role of PAM
item.description: StrongDM, which injects ephemeral, real-time credentials into developer workflows, will enable Delinea to offer privilege access management across cloud, SaaS, Kubernetes, and database environments.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163. "Although still relatively unspectacular, AI-generated malware such as Slopoly shows how easily threat actors can weaponize AI to develop new malware frameworks in a fraction of the time it used to take
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Stryker's Outage Is a Disaster Recovery Wake-Up Call
item.description: The Iranian cyberattack on Stryker is the kind of stress test that business continuity and disaster recovery programs often do not plan for.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: PixRevolution Malware Hijacks Brazil's PIX Transfers in Real Time
item.description: PixRevolution Android trojan hijacks Brazil’s PIX payments in real time using accessibility abuse

item.title: Critical Zero-Click Flaw in n8n Allows Full Server Compromise
item.description: The critical vulnerability affecting both cloud and self-hosted n8n instances requires no authentication or even n8n account to be exploited

item.title: What Orgs Can Learn From Olympics, World Cup IR Plans
item.description: In this edition of &quot;Reporters' Notebook,&quot; we discuss cyberattackers targeting the Milan-Cortina Winter Games, adding them to a long list of global sporting events in the crosshairs. Though the attack surface is grander, there are key incident-response takeaways for regular enterprises, too.
item.dc:creator: Tara Seals

item.title: Iran-Linked Hacktivists Hit Stryker, Knocking Employees Offline Across Multiple Countries
item.description: <p>A cyberattack disrupted global operations at medical device maker Stryker, knocking employees offline and raising concerns about destructive wiper attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-stryker-cyberattack-global-systems-disruption/">Iran-Linked Hacktivists Hit Stryker, Knocking Employees Offline Across Multiple Countries</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Cyber fallout from the Iran war: What to have on your radar
item.description: The cybersecurity implications of the war in the Middle East extend far beyond the region. Here’s where to focus your defenses.

item.title: Meta Rolls Out New Scam Alerts Across Facebook, WhatsApp, and Messenger
item.description: <p>Meta is rolling out new scam alerts across Facebook, WhatsApp, and Messenger as it ramps up AI-driven fraud detection and advertiser verification.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-meta-ai-scam-detection-facebook-messenger-whatsapp/">Meta Rolls Out New Scam Alerts Across Facebook, WhatsApp, and Messenger</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: How to Scale Phishing Detection in Your SOC: 3 Steps for CISOs
item.description: Phishing has quietly turned into one of the hardest enterprise threats to expose early. Instead of crude lures and obvious payloads, modern campaigns rely on trusted infrastructure, legitimate-looking authentication flows, and encrypted traffic that conceals malicious behavior from traditional detection layers. For CISOs, the priority is now clear: scale phishing detection in a way that helps
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: OAuth Trap, EDR Killer, Signal Phishing, Zombie ZIP, AI Platform Hack & More
item.description: Another Thursday, another pile of weird security stuff that somehow happened in just seven days. Some of it is clever. Some of it is lazy. A few bits fall into that uncomfortable category of “yeah… this is probably going to show up in real incidents sooner than we’d like.” The pattern this week feels familiar in a slightly annoying way. Old tricks are getting polished. New research shows how
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Issues Emergency Directive Over Exploited Cisco SD-WAN Flaws
item.description: CISA issued urgent directive as attackers exploit Cisco SD-WAN flaw granting admin access to networks

item.title: Attackers Don't Just Send Phishing Emails. They Weaponize Your SOC's Workload
item.description: The most dangerous phishing campaigns aren’t just designed to fool employees. Many are designed to exhaust the analysts investigating them. When a phishing investigation takes 12 hours instead of five minutes, the outcome can shift from a contained incident to a breach. For years, the cybersecurity industry has focused on the front door of phishing defense: employee training, email gateways that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Police Scotland Fined After Sharing Victim’s Phone Data
item.description: The ICO has fined Police Scotland after it shared the entire contents of a victim’s phone with her alleged attacker

item.title: Apple Issues Security Updates for Older iOS Devices Targeted by Coruna WebKit Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday backported fixes for a security flaw in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Sonoma to older versions after it was found to be used as part of the Coruna exploit kit. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2023-43010, relates to an unspecified vulnerability in WebKit that could result in memory corruption when processing maliciously crafted web content. The iPhone maker said the issue was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran Claims Massive Cyber-Attack on MedTech Firm Stryker
item.description: The pro-Iran Handala group claims to have wiped 200,000 systems in destructive wiper malware attack on US firm Stryker

item.title: Six Android Malware Families Target Pix Payments, Banking Apps, and Crypto Wallets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered half-a-dozen new Android malware families that come with capabilities to steal data from compromised devices and conduct financial fraud. The Android malware range from traditional banking trojans like PixRevolution, TaxiSpy RAT, BeatBanker, Mirax, and Oblivion RAT to full-fledged remote administration tools such as SURXRAT. PixRevolution, according to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited n8n RCE Bug as 24,700 Instances Remain Exposed
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical security flaw impacting n8n to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-68613 (CVSS score: 9.9), concerns a case of expression injection that leads to remote code execution. The security shortcoming was patched
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: A Guy Who Wrote the Code Died in 2005. I Still Have to Secure It
item.description: The real front line of American cybersecurity is a bidding war on eBay for 30-year-old industrial controllers.
item.dc:creator: Chuck Everette

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description: Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Patch Alert: Microsoft Fixes Nearly 80 Bugs, Including Critical Office Flaws
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s March Patch Tuesday fixes 78 vulnerabilities, including Office preview pane flaws, an Excel Copilot data leak risk, and an AI-discovered 9.8 severity bug.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-march-patch-tuesday-78-vulnerabilities/">Patch Alert: Microsoft Fixes Nearly 80 Bugs, Including Critical Office Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: ‘Agents of Chaos’: New Study Shows AI Agents Can Leak Data, Be Easily Manipulated
item.description: <p>As enterprise AI agent adoption accelerates, a new study exposes a governance gap that leaves most organizations unable to stop their own systems</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-agents-security-risks-governance/">‘Agents of Chaos’: New Study Shows AI Agents Can Leak Data, Be Easily Manipulated</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Xygeni GitHub Action Compromised Via Tag Poison
item.description: Attackers operated an active C2 implant for up to a week and compromised AppSec vendor Xygeni's xygeni/xygeni-action in that time.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: France: National Cybersecurity Agency Reports Ransomware Attack Drop in 2025
item.description: French small and medium businesses remained the organizations most targeted by ransomware in 2025

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey to phishing and scam traps. The attack, at its core, takes advantage of AI browsers' tendency to reason their actions and use it against the model itself to lower their security guardrails, Guardio
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
